subject: Project Management
title: Reference Class Forecasting
description: A micro-lesson that makes Reference Class Forecasting usable.

hook.text: Instead of asking 'how long will our project take?' ask 'how long did similar projects actually take?'

buildup.text: Reference class forecasting uses data from past, comparable projects to predict future outcomes.

discovery.text: Kahneman recommended it as the antidote to the inside view: replace stories with statistics.

twist.text: The UK government adopted reference class forecasting for all major projects after Flyvbjerg's advocacy.

climax.text: It works — but teams resist it because statistics are less flattering than their optimistic plans.

punchline.text: Don't plan by hope. Plan by history.

quiz.question: What is reference class forecasting?
options: Using data from comparable past projects to predict future outcomes
